name: Widen (Acquia DAM) Digital Asset Management API
what_it_does: Widen (now Acquia DAM) digital asset management REST API for marketing, creative, and brand teams to manage and distribute digital assets with metadata, rights management, and deep integration with web content management systems. Enables AI agents to manage asset upload, versioning, and metadata management for digital content repository automation, handle asset search and retrieval with faceted filtering for content delivery automation, access asset rights and expiry management for usage rights automation, retrieve asset collections and portals for campaign and brand asset management automation, manage asset derivatives and format conversion for multi-format delivery automation, handle asset analytics and usage tracking for content performance automation, access brand portal and asset sharing for partner and agency distribution automation, retrieve asset approval and workflow management for creative ops automation, manage product information and marketing content linking for DAM-PIM integration automation, and integrate Widen/Acquia DAM with Drupal, Sitecore, WordPress, Salesforce, and commerce platforms for end-to-end content operations automation.
use_cases: Retrieving brand assets from content management system agents
Managing asset rights from brand governance agents
Distributing campaign assets from marketing content agents
Linking assets to products from PIM integration agents
not_for: Small business basic file storage (enterprise DAM pricing)
Video streaming and hosting (use Wistia or Vimeo for streaming)
Code repository management (use GitHub or GitLab)
best_when: An enterprise or mid-market brand team wanting AI agents to automate asset management, rights tracking, multi-channel content distribution, and CMS integration through Widen/Acquia DAM's enterprise digital asset management platform.
avoid_when: ACQUIA ACQUISITION MIGRATION ROADMAP: Widen was acquired by Acquia in 2021; Acquia is integrating Widen into Acquia DAM product line; automated workflow planning must account for potential API endpoint migration during Acquia platform consolidation; monitor Acquia migration communications for API endpoint changes. METADATA TAXONOMY GOVERNANCE FOR AUTOMATED TAGGING: Widen metadata is governed by configurable taxonomy; automated asset tagging must use approved taxonomy values; automated tagging with non-controlled vocabulary terms creates tag pollution that degrades search quality; establish taxonomy governance before automated tagging deployment. ASSET PORTAL CONFIGURATION DEPENDENCY FOR SHARING: Widen asset portals for external sharing are configured per use case; automated asset distribution via portals requires pre-configured portal for each distribution context; automated asset sharing to external partners using unconfigured portal creates access control failure.

auth.notes: Widen/Acquia DAM uses OAuth 2.0 and API key for integrations. REST API with JSON. Madison, Wisconsin HQ (Widen); Boston, Massachusetts HQ (Acquia). Widen founded 1948; digital DAM product launched 2000. Acquired by Acquia in 2021. Acquia backed by Vista Equity Partners. Products: Acquia DAM (formerly Widen Collective), brand portal, asset analytics. CMS integrations: Drupal (native via Acquia), Sitecore, WordPress, Adobe Experience Manager. GDPR. SOC2. ISO27001. Competes with Bynder, Canto, and Brandfolder for enterprise DAM.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise annual subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per user and storage tier; custom enterprise pricing; implementation services required
pricing.notes: Madison WI / Boston MA. Acquia (Vista Equity) owned. Per-user annual subscription. Enterprise pricing. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; asset and metadata queries throttled; webhooks for asset upload, approval, and sharing events

known_agent_gotchas: ASSET FILE KEY vs ASSET ID FOR API OPERATIONS: Widen assets have both a file key (used in older API) and an asset ID (used in newer API); automated workflows must use consistent identifier type for asset operations; mixing file key and asset ID in automated workflows creates asset not found errors for operations expecting different identifier type

COLLECTION HIERARCHY FOR ASSET ORGANIZATION: Widen collections can be nested in multi-level hierarchy; automated asset organization must correctly navigate collection hierarchy for asset placement; automated asset filing in top-level collection instead of subcollection creates asset organization inconsistency with DAM taxonomy

DERIVATIVE GENERATION ASYNC PROCESSING: Widen derivative (thumbnail, web resolution) generation after new asset upload is asynchronous; automated asset distribution that immediately requests derivatives after upload may receive processing incomplete response; implement derivative availability check before automated downstream delivery

RIGHTS FIELD CUSTOM CONFIGURATION: Widen rights management fields are configurable per customer; rights field names and values vary across Widen implementations; automated rights checking must use customer-specific rights field configuration rather than generic field name assumptions

DRUPAL CONNECTOR FOR ACQUIA SITES: Widen/Acquia DAM has native Drupal connector for Acquia DX Platform sites; automated Drupal content publishing with asset embedding should use native Drupal connector rather than REST API for better content workflow integration; REST API is for non-Drupal integrations
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise DAM. SOC2, GDPR, ISO27001. OAuth2/API key. US/EU. Brand assets, rights, and content data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise DAM. SOC2, GDPR, ISO27001. OAuth2/API key. US/EU. Brand asset and rights data.
